title: broadcast("", ...) uses empty string sentinel to include sender
description: What is awkward: state.rs broadcast() excludes the sender by comparing entry.key() != from_id. In ws.rs the nickname assignment broadcasts to ALL clients including sender using state.broadcast("", RelayMessage::UserNickname{...}). The empty string is a sentinel meaning 'no exclusion'. This works because PubIds are 64-char hex strings, but the mechanism is implicit. A future contributor adding a new broadcast call who doesn't know this convention will use their own pub_id as from_id and accidentally exclude themselves. Suggested rewrite: change broadcast signature to broadcast(exclude: Option<&str>, msg) with None meaning 'send to all', Some(id) meaning 'exclude this id'. This makes the intent explicit and is a one-line match change in the filter closure.
